mnemonics.title: CATTLE Acronym
mnemonics.content:
C - Commodity (interchangeable)
A - Automated (self-healing)
T - Trivial to replace
T - Treated as identical
L - Launched from templates
E - Ephemeral (short-lived)

mnemonics.title: PETS Acronym
mnemonics.content:
P - Personal (named individually)
E - Expensive to maintain
T - Tended by humans
S - Specialized (unique config)

mnemonics.title: Cattle Phrases
mnemonics.content:
When cattle gets sick - REPLACE (not repair)
Kill it and spawn another
Snowflakes to cattle
Pets die, cattle are culled

acronyms.usage: Defining infrastructure in code to enable automated, consistent deployment of cattle-style servers
Target time to restore service after failure - much lower with cattle model due to automation
Maximum data loss measured in time - important for designing backup strategies in cattle model
Automated pipelines that build and deploy cattle-style infrastructure changes

visualTips.visualize: Picture a farm: some animals have names and personal barns (pets), while the rest are identical numbered cattle in a herd (cattle). Only the named ones get special treatment when sick.
Think of taking a photo (immutable snapshot) of a server and deploying copies. Any changes require a new photo, not editing the existing one.
Adding more lanes to a highway (horizontal) vs making one lane wider (vertical). More lanes = more throughput, no single point of failure.
A hotel with infinite rooms that automatically open or close based on demand, with guests moved to any available room - identical, replaceable rooms.

analogies.analogy: Like a beloved family dog (Pet) vs cattle on a ranch (Cattle). When the dog gets sick, you take it to the vet. When cattle get sick, you remove them from the herd and bring in healthy ones.
Airlines treat seats like cattle - all seats in economy class are identical. When one seat is broken, you move the passenger to another identical seat, not rebuild the broken one.
Car manufacturing uses immutable designs - each car is built from a spec. Changes require new assembly instructions, not modifying already-built cars.
A restaurant adds more identical tables during rush hour (horizontal). They don't make existing tables bigger (vertical). Each table serves the same way.
